ourine	Divide or Conquer	Seems to be a question about the word "reversal" in the sense of "reversing" or "undoing" something. I don't think that's the case here. The word "reversal" is used to describe the situation where a trial court, or a panel of judges, is asked to review a decision made by a lower court. If the trial court agrees with the decision, it will affirm the decision. If it disagrees, it will reverse the decision. It doesn't undo the decision. It simply reviews the decision for errors of law or fact

es>Dozens of businesses are reported to have been attacked by a new malware that has been dubbed "Petya". The malware encrypts hard drives on Windows computers, changes the extension of encrypted files to ".ccc" and leaves ransom notes in every folder containing encrypted files. The ransom note is a fake message, supposedly from a group called "Ground Zero Crew". In fact, the malware does not encrypt any files but only renames them. Most likely, the malware is a work of the creator of the Petya ransomware who is trying to use a new approach to make the malware harder to detect for antivirus software. In
